name: Yun Wu
region: Various high-altitude regions, China (most famous: Lushan, Jiangxi; also Zhejiang, Sichuan, others)
terroir: High-altitude mountains (600-1500m) with persistent cloud cover — the name literally means 'cloud mist', describing the growing conditions
flavor_profile: A common name across China for locally produced green teas grown in misty, high-altitude conditions. The cloud cover acts as a natural shade, boosting amino acid content and reducing bitterness. The general profile is bright and vegetal with a clean sweetness, moderate body, and a refreshing finish. Quality and character vary significantly by origin — Lushan Yun Wu from Jiangxi is the most famous example. Good daily-drinking greens that offer honest quality without premium pricing.

brewing.tips: A forgiving tea style that works well across a range of parameters. Good for gongfu beginners learning green tea technique.
